content.full_text:
RAVI ‘lEHTA
c.
I A NM A N
FAIR
POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
February 6,
1996
Mr.
Robert D.
Thornton
Nossaman, Guthner,
Knox
& Elliott
18101 Von Karman Avenue,
Suite 1800
Post Office Box 19772
Irvine, California
92713—9772
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No.
A—96—017
Dear Mr.
Thornton:
This is in response to your request for advice regarding the
reporting provisions of the Political Reform Act (the “Act”).
1
QUESTION
Must Mr.
Gregory Henk,
Executive Vice President for Design
and Construction for the Transportation Corridor Agencies, report
on his annual statement of economic interests a housing loan
received from the agencies as part of a compensation package
previously disclosed by the agencies as a matter of public record?
CONCLUSION
No.
The secured housing loan which is part of his
compensation package is considered part of his government salary
under the Act.
Therefore, the official does not need to report
such a loan on his statement of economic interests.
FACTS
Your firm is counsel to the San Joaquin Hills Transportation
Corridor Agency and the Foothill/Eastern Transportation Corridor
Agency (collectively the “TCA”).
Mr.
Gregory Henk is the
1
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission
regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations,
Sections
18000—18995.
428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
(916) 322-5660


File No.
A—96—017
Page
2
Executive Vice President for Design and Construction for the TCA
and has been employed by the TCA since 1990.
As an employee,
he
is designated under the TCA’s conflict of interest code.
Mr.
Henk’s employment contract and compensation package
includes salary, health, vacation, retirement and other benefits
including a specified housing loan.
On April 9,
1990,
a
memorandum outlining Mr.
Henk’s compensation package was
transmitted by the Executive Director of the
TCA to the TCA Boards
of Directors,
and the compensation package was approved during a
regularly scheduled meeting of the Board of Directors on April 19,
1990.
The memorandum describing Mr.
Henk’s compensation package,
including the home loan,
was made available to members of the
public at the time of the meeting.
The memorandum was also made
available to the press and the Los Angeles Times subsequently
published an article about Mr. Henk’s compensation package,
including the home loan.
On October 8,
1992, the employment
contract was amended pursuant to TCA Board of Directors action and
disclosed to the public in the minutes of that meeting.
ANALYSIS
Under the Act,
loans are generally considered reportable
income.
(Sections 87207(l)(a),
(2)
and (5),
87302(b).)
However,
certain types of loans are not considered income under the Act and
are not reportable.
These include loans from a commercial lending
institution which are used to finance the individual’s principal
residence or loans which are under $10,000.
Such loans must be
made in the lender’s regular course of business on terms available
to members of the public without regard to official status.
(Section 82030(b)(8).)
Other loans which are not considered
income under the Act are loans from an individual’s family members
and credit card debt up to $10,000.
(Sections 82030(b) (9)
and
(10).)
Mr.
Henk’s home loan does not fit within these exemptions.
However, the Act’s definition of income also excludes:
(2)
Salary and reimbursement for expenses or per
diem received from a state, local,
or federal government
agency and reimbursement for travel expenses and per
diem received from a bona fide educational,
academic,
or
charitable organization.
***
(Section 82030(b) (2).)
The loan from TCA to Mr.
Henk, disclosed publicly in the
agency—adopted compensation package, is part of his overall
compensation from the TCA.
Consistent with prior Commission
advice, the loan would be considered part of Mr. Henk’s “salary”
from a government agency and therefore he would not be required to
report the loan on his statement of economic interests.
(Haughton
Advice Letter,
No.
A—84—256.)


File No.
A—96—017
Page
3
I trust this answers your question.
If you have any further
questions regarding this matter, please contact me at
916/322—5660.
Sincerely,
Steven C.
Churchwell
General Counsel
By:
Hyla P.
Wagner
Counsel,
Legal Division
SGC:HPW:ak

QUESTION: Must Mr.
Gregory Henk,
Executive Vice President for Design
and Construction for the Transportation Corridor Agencies, report
on his annual statement of economic interests a housing loan
received from the agencies as part of a compensation package
previously disclosed by the agencies as a matter of public record?

CONCLUSION: No.
The secured housing loan which is part of his
compensation package is considered part of his government salary
under the Act.
Therefore, the official does not need to report
such a loan on his statement of economic interests.
